name: useClickOutside
description: Registers a global click listener that fires a callback whenever a click occurs outside of the specified excluded elements.

args.name: excludedSelectors
args.description: Array of CSS selector strings. Clicks on elements matching any selector (or their ancestors) are ignored.

args.name: callback
args.description: Function called when a click occurs outside all excluded elements.

name: useColorScheme
description: Resolves the active color scheme for a component by merging the component-level prop, component-specific global configuration, and the global default — in that priority order.

args.name: component
args.description: Yes

args.name: colorScheme
args.description: Yes

name: useFocusable
description: Manages keyboard-accessible focus tracking within a wrapper element by querying `[data-focusable]` elements and updating a focus index with throttled mouse-move support.

args.name: wrapperElement
args.description: Template ref pointing to the container element that holds `[data-focusable]` children.

name: useInput
description: Central composable for form input components. Orchestrates validation rules, user-facing messages, form integration, disabled/readonly states, and lifecycle callbacks in a single call.

args.name: ctx
args.description: Component context providing the `emit` function.

args.name: inputParams
args.description: Object describing the input's reactive state and callbacks (see Types).

example:
<template>
    <vs-input-wrapper :id="computedId" :messages="computedMessages" :state="computedState">
        <input
            :id="computedId"
            v-model="inputValue"
            :disabled="computedDisabled"
            :readonly="computedReadonly"
            :class="{ 'vs-shake': shake }"
        />
    </vs-input-wrapper>
</template>

<script setup>
import { ref, toRefs } from 'vue';
import { useInput } from '@/composables';

const props = defineProps({
    modelValue: { type: String, default: '' },
    disabled: Boolean,
    readonly: Boolean,
    rules: { type: Array, default: () => [] },
    messages: { type: Array, default: () => [] },
    id: { type: String, default: '' },
    state: { type: String, default: 'idle' },
});

const emit = defineEmits(['update:modelValue', 'update:valid', 'update:changed', 'change']);
const { modelValue, disabled, readonly, rules, messages, id, state } = toRefs(props);

const inputValue = ref(props.modelValue);

const { computedId, computedDisabled, computedReadonly, computedMessages, computedState, validate, clear, shake } =
    useInput({ emit }, { inputValue, modelValue, disabled, readonly, rules, messages, id, state });
</script>

name: useInputForm
description: Connects an individual input component to the nearest parent `VsForm` via Vue's provide/inject mechanism, enabling form-level validation, clearing, and state synchronization.

args.name: id
args.description: Reactive ref holding the input's unique id used to register in the form.

args.name: valid
args.description: Reactive validity state of the input.

args.name: changed
args.description: Reactive ref indicating whether the user has changed the value.

args.name: validate
args.description: Validation function called when the form triggers validation.

args.name: clear
args.description: Clear function called when the form triggers a clear.

example:
<script setup>
import { ref, computed } from 'vue';
import { useInputForm } from '@/composables';

// Typically used internally by useInput
const id = computed(() => 'my-input-id');
const valid = ref(true);
const changed = ref(false);

function validate() { return valid.value; }
function clear() { changed.value = false; }

const { formDisabled, formReadonly } = useInputForm(id, valid, changed, validate, clear);
</script>

name: useInputMessages
description: Evaluates static and function-based messages for an input value and combines them with rule messages into a single reactive list.

args.name: inputValue
args.description: The current input value passed to function-based messages.

args.name: messages
args.description: Array of static `StateMessage` objects or functions returning a `StateMessage`.

args.name: ruleMessages
args.description: Array of messages produced by validation rules (from `useInputRules`).

example:
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { useInputMessages } from '@/composables';

const inputValue = ref('');
const messages = ref([
    { state: 'info', text: 'Enter your username.' },
    (val) => val.length > 10 ? { state: 'warning', text: 'Too long' } : null,
]);
const ruleMessages = ref([]);

const { computedMessages, showRuleMessages, checkMessages } = useInputMessages(inputValue, messages, ruleMessages);
</script>

name: useInputOption
description: Keeps a selection-based input value in sync with its options list. When the options change, any selected value that no longer exists in the new list is cleared automatically.

args.name: inputValue
args.description: The current selected value (or array of values when `multiple` is true).

args.name: options
args.description: The full list of available options.

args.name: optionLabel
args.description: Key path to extract the display label from an option object.

args.name: optionValue
args.description: Key path to extract the value from an option object.

args.name: multiple
args.description: When `true`, `inputValue` is treated as an array and filtered on change.

example:
<script setup>
import { ref, toRefs } from 'vue';
import { useInputOption } from '@/composables';

const inputValue = ref(null);
const options = ref([{ label: 'Apple', value: 'apple' }, { label: 'Banana', value: 'banana' }]);
const optionLabel = ref('label');
const optionValue = ref('value');

const { getOptionLabel, getOptionValue } = useInputOption(inputValue, options, optionLabel, optionValue);
</script>

name: useInputRules
description: Evaluates an array of synchronous and asynchronous validation rules against the current input value, collecting error messages into a reactive list.

args.name: inputValue
args.description: The current input value passed to each rule function.

args.name: rules
args.description: User-supplied validation rules.

args.name: defaultRules
args.description: Built-in rules provided by the component (e.g. required, type checks).

args.name: noDefaultRules
args.description: When `true`, only `rules` are applied; `defaultRules` are ignored.

example:
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { useInputRules } from '@/composables';

const inputValue = ref('');
const rules = ref([
    (val) => val.length >= 3 || 'Minimum 3 characters required',
    async (val) => {
        const taken = await checkIfUsernameTaken(val);
        return taken ? 'Username already taken' : '';
    },
]);
const defaultRules = ref([]);
const noDefaultRules = ref(false);

const { ruleMessages, checkRules } = useInputRules(inputValue, rules, defaultRules, noDefaultRules);
</script>

name: useIndexSelector
description: Manages index-based selection for a list of items with optional per-item or global disabled support, keyboard navigation, and edge detection.

args.name: list
args.description: Reactive array of items to select from.

args.name: disabled
args.description: `undefined`

name: useOptionLabelValue
description: Extracts display labels and underlying values from arbitrary option objects using configurable key paths.

args.name: optionLabel
args.description: Key path used to extract the display label from an option object.

args.name: optionValue
args.description: Key path used to extract the value from an option object.

example:
<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { useOptionLabelValue } from '@/composables';

const optionLabel = ref('name');
const optionValue = ref('id');

const { getOptionLabel, getOptionValue } = useOptionLabelValue(optionLabel, optionValue);

const option = { id: 1, name: 'Alice' };
console.log(getOptionLabel(option)); // 'Alice'
console.log(getOptionValue(option)); // 1
</script>

name: useOptionList
description: Transforms a raw options array into a normalized, computed list with stable ids, extracted labels/values, and per-item disabled state.

args.name: options
args.description: Raw array of option items.

args.name: optionLabel
args.description: Key path for extracting the display label.

args.name: optionValue
args.description: Key path for extracting the value.

args.name: disabled
args.default: ((option: any, index: number, options: any[]) => boolean)>
args.description: Yes

name: useOverlayCallback
description: Manages the active/inactive lifecycle of an overlay component and registers its callbacks in the global overlay callback store so other parts of the app can trigger them.

args.name: id
args.description: Reactive ref for the overlay id. Falls back to an auto-generated id when empty.

args.name: callbacks
args.description: Object containing optional lifecycle callbacks for the overlay.

name: useOverlayDom
description: Creates and appends a positioned overlay `<div>` element to a target DOM element, with configurable size, pointer events, and z-index.
example:
<script setup>
import { onMounted } from 'vue';
import { useOverlayDom } from '@/composables';

const { appendOverlayDom } = useOverlayDom();

onMounted(() => {
    const container = document.querySelector('.vs-select');
    appendOverlayDom(container, '#vs-select-overlay', {
        zIndex: 6000,
        pointerEvents: 'auto',
    });
});
</script>

name: usePositioning
description: Computes and maintains the absolute pixel position of an attachment element relative to a target CSS selector, with automatic viewport-overflow correction, scroll/resize tracking, and ResizeObserver support.

args.name: target
args.description: CSS selector string identifying the target element to position against.

args.name: attachment
args.description: Template ref of the element to be positioned.

name: useScrollLock
description: Prevents scrolling on a container element (defaults to `body`) by setting `overflow: hidden` and compensating for scrollbar width on non-touch devices.

args.name: container
args.description: CSS selector for the element to lock scrolling on.

example:
<script setup>
import { useScrollLock } from '@/composables';

const { isLocked, lock, unlock } = useScrollLock('body');

function openModal() {
    lock();
}

function closeModal() {
    unlock();
}
</script>

name: useStateClass
description: Converts a reactive `UIState` value into the corresponding CSS class objects for component state styling.

args.name: state
args.description: Reactive ref holding the current UI state of the component.

name: useStringModifier
description: Applies reactive string transformation modifiers (`capitalize`, `lower`, `upper`) to a string value.

args.name: modifiers
args.description: Reactive object with optional boolean modifier flags.

example:
<template>
    <input v-model="inputValue" @input="onInput" />
</template>

<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { useStringModifier } from '@/composables';

const modifiers = ref({ upper: true });
const { modifyStringValue } = useStringModifier(modifiers);

const inputValue = ref('');
function onInput(e) {
    inputValue.value = modifyStringValue(e.target.value);
}
</script>

name: useStyleSet
description: Resolves, merges, and transforms a component's style-set into both a typed `componentStyleSet` object and a flat `styleSetVariables` CSS custom property map.

args.name: component
args.description: Yes

args.name: baseStyleSet
args.description: Default style-set applied before the user prop (lowest priority).

args.name: additionalStyleSet
args.description: Dynamic overrides derived from other component props (highest priority).

example:
<template>
    <button
        :class="['vs-button', colorSchemeClass]"
        :style="{ ...styleSetVariables, ...componentStyleSet.component }"
    >
        <vs-loading v-if="loading" :style-set="componentStyleSet.loading" />
        <slot />
    </button>
</template>

<script setup>
import { ref, toRefs } from 'vue';
import { useStyleSet } from '@/composables';

const props = defineProps({ styleSet: [String, Object] });
const { styleSet } = toRefs(props);

const { componentStyleSet, styleSetVariables } = useStyleSet('VsButton', styleSet);
</script>

name: useValueMatcher
description: Manages toggle-style value matching for checkbox and switch components, supporting both single-value and multiple-value (array) modes with configurable `trueValue` and `falseValue`.

args.name: multiple
args.description: When `true`, `inputValue` is treated as an array and `trueValue` is added/removed.

args.name: inputValue
args.description: The reactive current value of the input (single value or array).

args.name: trueValue
args.description: The value representing the "checked" / "on" state.

args.name: falseValue
args.description: The value representing the "unchecked" / "off" state.

example:
<template>
    <input
        type="checkbox"
        :checked="isMatched"
        @change="toggle"
    />
</template>

<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue';
import { useValueMatcher } from '@/composables';

const multiple = ref(false);
const inputValue = ref(false);
const trueValue = ref(true);
const falseValue = ref(false);

const { isMatched, getUpdatedValue, getClearedValue } = useValueMatcher(multiple, inputValue, trueValue, falseValue);

function toggle(e) {
    inputValue.value = getUpdatedValue(e.target.checked);
}
</script>
